strategy: Krampus punishes setup hard: casting a blade may trigger Steal Charm (he takes ALL your charms), and Global spells trigger it too. Minimize blades and hit the same round you blade so he cannot steal first.
Feint is a trap here; he Cleanse Wards twice on a Feint, which defeats Indemnity. Skip Feint and rely on hit-round blades, auras, or a Sun-enchanted big hit.
Every third round he Dimension Shifts his own traps onto a random friend or foe, so traps you place can vanish or bounce. Time traps to hit before a third-round shift.
He is Fire; bring a non-Fire hit or prism.
At Round 5 he summons Chilling and Stormy Helpers and resummons them when killed. Do not grind the adds; burst Krampus down. Join on time to avoid the late-arrival Scald and Smoke Mantle.
cheatNotes: Late arrival eats a Shadow Scald plus a -75% Smoke Mantle. Zone in on time.
Casting a blade can trigger Steal Charm, taking ALL your charms. Blade only in the same round you hit so he cannot steal them first.
He recasts this steal at random through the fight, so never leave charms sitting for a full round.
Global spells also trigger the charm-steal. Avoid dropping globals mid-setup.
Casting Feint makes him Cleanse Ward twice, which beats Indemnity. Do not Feint him; use other setup.
Every third round he Dimension Shifts his traps onto a random friend or foe. Land your hit before the shift or the traps scatter.
At Round 5 he summons Chilling and Stormy Helpers. Do not grind them; pressure Krampus.
Killed helpers resummon after one round, so racing Krampus beats clearing adds.
